dialogue: You did it! In this lesson, you learned the notes of the piano, how half steps and whole steps work, the major scale pattern ГўВҖВ” Whole, Whole, Half, Whole, Whole, Whole, Half ГўВҖВ” and you played the entire C Major scale. ThatГўВҖВҷs a huge deal for your first lesson!
title: The C Major Scale

C_HALFWHOLE.label: Can identify half steps and whole steps

moments.dialogue: Hey there! Welcome to your very first lesson at the School of Motes-Art. IГўВҖВҷm Motes-Art, your music coach, and today weГўВҖВҷre going to learn something really cool ГўВҖВ” the C Major Scale. ItГўВҖВҷs the foundation of everything in music. You ready?
No worries ГўВҖВ” take your time. Whenever youГўВҖВҷre ready, just say ГўВҖВҳreadyГўВҖВҷ or ГўВҖВҳletГўВҖВҷs goГўВҖВҷ and weГўВҖВҷll get started.

Awesome! Before we jump in ГўВҖВ” do you have your keyboard or piano in front of you? If you do, go ahead and place your hands on the keys. If not, no worries, you can follow along and weГўВҖВҷll still make it work.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Make sure your wrists are relaxed and your fingers are slightly curved.

moments.dialogue: Now I need to teach you the secret code behind every scale in music. ItГўВҖВҷs all about half steps and whole steps. A half step is the distance from one key to the very next key ГўВҖВ” no skipping. If you go from a white key to the black key right next to it? ThatГўВҖВҷs a half step. From one black key to the white key right next to it? Also a half step. ItГўВҖВҷs the smallest step you can take on a piano.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Try playing two keys right next to each other.

auditory.addDialogue: Listen to how close they sound ГўВҖВ” almost like theyГўВҖВҷre bumping into each other.

moments.dialogue: Now, a whole step is just two half steps put together. You skip one key. So if you start on C and go up a whole step, you skip over the black key and land on D. That skip? ThatГўВҖВҷs a whole step. Think of it like this: half step is a tiny step, whole step is a regular step.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Try it ГўВҖВ” press C, skip a key, press D.

readwrite.addDialogue: Half = 1 key apart. Whole = 2 keys apart.

moments.dialogue: LetГўВҖВҷs start with the basics. Look at the piano ГўВҖВ” you see white keys and black keys, right? The white keys are named after the first seven letters of the alphabet: A, B, C, D, E, F, G. And then it repeats. ThatГўВҖВҷs it ГўВҖВ” just seven letter names for all of music.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Try pressing each white key as I say the name.

moments.dialogue: Now, hereГўВҖВҷs something important ГўВҖВ” finding middle C. See those groups of two black keys? Find a group of two black keys near the middle of your keyboard. The white key just to the left of those two black keys ГўВҖВ” thatГўВҖВҷs C. ThatГўВҖВҷs where everything starts. Go ahead and press it if you can.

midi.feedback: ThatГўВҖВҷs it! You found middle C.
Close ГўВҖВ” look for the group of two black keys. C is the white key just to the left of them.

moments.dialogue: Look at the black keys ГўВҖВ” they come in groups of two and three. Find a group of two near the middle. The white key directly to the left of that group is C. Try again!

Great. Now letГўВҖВҷs talk about your fingers. In piano, we number our fingers one through five. Your thumb is one, your pointer finger is two, middle finger is three, ring finger is four, and your pinky is five. Right hand, place your thumb ГўВҖВ” finger one ГўВҖВ” on that middle C.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Wiggle each finger as I count ГўВҖВ” one, two, three, four, five.

readwrite.addDialogue: Remember: thumb is always one, pinky is always five.

midi.feedback: Perfect ГўВҖВ” thumb on C, youГўВҖВҷre set.

moments.dialogue: Quick check ГўВҖВ” if I start on E and go to the very next key, which is F, is that a half step or a whole step?

correctResponse.feedback: ThatГўВҖВҷs right! E to F is a half step ГўВҖВ” thereГўВҖВҷs no black key between them.

wrongResponse.feedback: Not quite ГўВҖВ” look closely. E and F are right next to each other, no key in between. That makes it a half step. Remember: half step means the very next key.

moments.dialogue: HereГўВҖВҷs a hint ГўВҖВ” is there any key between E and F? Look at the keyboard. If thereГўВҖВҷs nothing between them, theyГўВҖВҷre as close as two keys can be.
Nice. Now what about C to D? I skip over that black key ГўВҖВ” is that a half step or a whole step?

correctResponse.feedback: Exactly! We skipped a key, so thatГўВҖВҷs a whole step. YouГўВҖВҷre getting it.

wrongResponse.feedback: Close, but remember ГўВҖВ” we skipped over that black key between C and D. Skipping a key means itГўВҖВҷs a whole step.

moments.dialogue: Okay, now hereГўВҖВҷs the magic. Every major scale in music follows the exact same pattern of whole steps and half steps. ItГўВҖВҷs like a secret code. Ready? The pattern is: Whole, Whole, Half, Whole, Whole, Whole, Half. Let me say that one more time ГўВҖВ” Whole, Whole, Half... Whole, Whole, Whole, Half. That pattern builds every single major scale.

readwrite.addDialogue: Two groups: three and four. W-W-H then W-W-W-H.

auditory.addDialogue: Listen to the rhythm: whole whole HALF ГўВҖВ” whole whole whole HALF.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Tap your knee for whole, snap for half. Ready? Tap, tap, snap. Tap, tap, tap, snap.

moments.dialogue: And hereГўВҖВҷs why C Major is the best one to start with ГўВҖВ” it uses only white keys! Watch: start on C. Whole step to D. Whole step to E. Half step to F ГўВҖВ” see how E and F are right next to each other, no black key? Then: whole step to G. Whole step to A. Whole step to B. Half step back to C. And weГўВҖВҷre home.

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Play each key as I name it.

auditory.suffix: Now listen to the whole scale as one smooth phrase.

moments.dialogue: Let me see if you caught the pattern. The major scale goes: Whole, Whole... then what comes next?

correctResponse.feedback: Yes! Whole, Whole, Half ГўВҖВ” and then Whole, Whole, Whole, Half. YouГўВҖВҷve got the code!

wrongResponse.feedback: Good try ГўВҖВ” but remember, the pattern has two groups. The first group is: Whole, Whole, Half. That half step is the surprise! ItГўВҖВҷs what makes it sound like a scale and not just random notes.

microGame.description: Student fills in remaining pattern slots by tapping or speaking W/H

moments.dialogue: Think of it in two groups. Group one has three: Whole, Whole, Half. Now try again ГўВҖВ” Whole, Whole, then what?
HereГўВҖВҷs a clue ГўВҖВ” itГўВҖВҷs the shorter one. A half step. Whole, Whole... Half! ThatГўВҖВҷs the first group of the pattern.
Let me play it for you. Listen carefully and watch the keys light up. HereГўВҖВҷs the C Major scale going up...

auditory.suffix: Now listen to it one more time with the note names.

moments.dialogue: Now let me show you something cool about the fingering. You start with finger one ГўВҖВ” your thumb ГўВҖВ” on C. Then two on D, three on E. Now hereГўВҖВҷs the trick: your thumb crosses under to play F. Then two on G, three on A, four on B, and five ГўВҖВ” your pinky ГўВҖВ” lands on the high C. Thumb cross is the secret move!

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Try it now ГўВҖВ” 1, 2, 3, cross under, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

moments.dialogue: And coming back down? Just reverse it. C, B, A, G, F, E, D, C. Same notes, same pattern, just backwards. Listen to how it sounds going down...
Your turn! Start on middle C with your thumb ГўВҖВ” finger one ГўВҖВ” and play the scale going up. Take your time. IГўВҖВҷll be right here if you need help.

allCorrect.feedback: Beautiful! That was the C Major scale. How did that feel?

partialCorrect.feedback: Good effort! You got most of it. LetГўВҖВҷs see where you got tripped up.

moments.dialogue: No worries ГўВҖВ” letГўВҖВҷs try again, slower this time. Start on C. IГўВҖВҷll light up each target note for you.
That was great. Now, while you were playing ГўВҖВ” did you notice where it felt like the notes were really close together? Those two spots where thereГўВҖВҷs no black key between the white keys?

correctResponse.feedback: Exactly! E to F and B to C ГўВҖВ” those are your two half steps in C Major. Everything else is a whole step.

wrongResponse.feedback: No worries. Look at the keyboard ГўВҖВ” find two white keys with no black key between them. Those are your half steps. E to F is one, B to C is the other.

moments.dialogue: LetГўВҖВҷs do it one more time. Play the C Major scale going up, and this time, try to say the pattern out loud as you play ГўВҖВ” ГўВҖВҳwhole, whole, half, whole, whole, whole, half.ГўВҖВҷ Ready? Go!

allCorrect.feedback: YouГўВҖВҷre making this look easy! ThatГўВҖВҷs the whole C Major scale with the pattern.

microGame.description: Flash each stepГўВҖВҷs W/H label, student must repeat sequence from memory

moments.dialogue: Alright, letГўВҖВҷs see what you remember! Can you tell me all the notes in the C Major scale, from C to the next C?

match: c d e f g a b c
correctResponse.feedback: Perfect! C, D, E, F, G, A, B, C. You nailed it.

wrongResponse.feedback: Almost! Let me help ГўВҖВ” C, D, E, F, G, A, B, C. The musical alphabet starting on C.

moments.dialogue: One more ГўВҖВ” whatГўВҖВҷs the pattern for building a major scale? The whole steps and half steps?

match: whole whole half whole whole whole half
w w h w w w h
correctResponse.feedback: ThatГўВҖВҷs the code! You can build any major scale with that pattern.

wrongResponse.feedback: Let me help you remember: Whole, Whole, Half ГўВҖВ” pause ГўВҖВ” Whole, Whole, Whole, Half. Two groups!

moments.dialogue: No worries at all ГўВҖВ” letГўВҖВҷs go through the notes one more time. Think of it as the alphabet, but starting on C: C, D, E, F, G, A, B, and back to C. Just seven different notes, then it repeats. LetГўВҖВҷs say them together ГўВҖВ” C... D... E... F... G... A... B... C!

auditory.addDialogue: Listen to each note sing its name.

readwrite.addOverlay: C ГўВҶВ’ D ГўВҶВ’ E ГўВҶВ’ F ГўВҶВ’ G ГўВҶВ’ A ГўВҶВ’ B ГўВҶВ’ C

moments.dialogue: LetГўВҖВҷs make sure weГўВҖВҷve got half steps and whole steps locked in. HereГўВҖВҷs the easiest way to remember: look at the piano. If two keys are right next to each other with nothing in between ГўВҖВ” thatГўВҖВҷs a half step. If thereГўВҖВҷs a key you skip over in between ГўВҖВ” thatГўВҖВҷs a whole step. Simple as that.

The pattern ГўВҖВ” letГўВҖВҷs try a trick to remember it. Think of it as two groups. Group one: Whole, Whole, Half. Group two: Whole, Whole, Whole, Half. Three in the first group, four in the second. Say it with me ГўВҖВ” group one: Whole, Whole, Half. Group two: Whole, Whole, Whole, Half.

auditory.addDialogue: LetГўВҖВҷs chant it: whole whole HALF ГўВҖВ” whole whole whole HALF!

kinesthetic.addDialogue: Clap for whole, snap for half. Ready?

readwrite.addOverlay: Group 1: W-W-H (3 steps) | Group 2: W-W-W-H (4 steps)

microGame.description: Student repeats the pattern verbally, speech recognition checks

moments.dialogue: Last round ГўВҖВ” letГўВҖВҷs play the full C Major scale, up and back down. C all the way up to the high C, then come back down to where you started. Show me what youГўВҖВҷve learned!

perfect.feedback: Yes! That was incredible. You just played the C Major scale up AND down. ThatГўВҖВҷs a real musician move.

ascending_only.feedback: The way up was perfect! Coming back down takes a little more practice, but youГўВҖВҷre almost there.

partial.feedback: YouГўВҖВҷre getting there! That was a solid effort.

moments.dialogue: ThatГўВҖВҷs okay! Going up and down is the hardest part. You know the notes, you know the pattern ГўВҖВ” the rest is just practice. YouГўВҖВҷre doing great for your first lesson.

You did it! In this lesson, you learned the notes of the piano, how half steps and whole steps work, the major scale pattern ГўВҖВ” Whole, Whole, Half, Whole, Whole, Whole, Half ГўВҖВ” and you played the entire C Major scale. ThatГўВҖВҷs a huge deal for your first lesson!

Next time, weГўВҖВҷre going to use what you learned today to start building more scales and chords. The C Major scale is the key that unlocks everything else. So keep practicing it ГўВҖВ” even five minutes a day makes a difference.
IГўВҖВҷm proud of you. You showed up, you learned something new, and that takes real effort. IГўВҖВҷll see you next time. Keep making music!
